chapter_title: POEM: Not It Never Comes Again (Memorization)
text:
Read the poem answer the questions that follow;  
You are old, Father William the young man said
And your hair has become very white;
And yet you incessantly stand on your head
Do you think add your age it is right?
In my youth father William reply to his son,
I feared it might injure the brain,
But now that I’m perfectly sure I have none,
Why I do it gay again and again.

chapter_title: POEM: Not It Never Comes Again (Memorization)
text:
Develop the story using the clues given below.
Once in a forest a hare __ tortoise were friends. Hare made fun. The tortoise ________ challenge ________ Had  a
race. Hare ran fast. Tortoise ________ slow. Hare took rest ________ slept under tree. Tortoise ________ moving slowly.
Tortoise won the race.
